marketEvidence.spreadClaim: Real quotes for Rock Hill -> Charlotte (45 mi, standard service) ranged from $1,158 to $6,506 — a 5.6x spread for identical work.

sources.name: Challenge brief price survey
sources.note: Rock Hill -> Charlotte, 45 mi, standard 2BR service

sources.name: FMCSA
sources.note: Sight-unseen estimates are 40% more likely to blow up on moving day

sources.name: BBB
sources.note: 13,000+ complaints/negative reviews against movers per year, largely unexpected price hikes

sources.name: moveBuddha / industry rate cards
sources.note: Local crew-hour rates and mileage conventions

interviewQuestions.ask: Where are you moving from, and where to?
interviewQuestions.why: Mileage and crew travel time drive the base.

interviewQuestions.ask: What date are you targeting, and is it flexible by a day or two?
interviewQuestions.why: Weekend and month-end dates carry surcharges that are negotiable.

interviewQuestions.ask: How many bedrooms, and roughly how full are they?
interviewQuestions.why: Bedrooms map to crew size and truck hours.

interviewQuestions.ask: Walk me through the large items — beds, sofas, dressers, appliances, anything over about 100 pounds.
interviewQuestions.why: Large or special items are the number one source of day-of surprises.

interviewQuestions.ask: Anything unusual — a piano, a gun safe, a treadmill, a fish tank, a large TV?
interviewQuestions.why: Special items carry named fees that must be quoted upfront.

interviewQuestions.ask: At the pickup address, what floor are you on, and is there an elevator?
interviewQuestions.why: Stairs and long carries are the most common add-on fees.

interviewQuestions.ask: And at the new place — what floor, elevator or stairs?
How far is the truck able to park from the door at each end?
interviewQuestions.why: Over 75 feet typically triggers a long-carry charge.

interviewQuestions.ask: Do you want them packing for you, partial packing, or are you packing everything yourself?
interviewQuestions.why: Packing is the biggest upsell lever in the industry.

interviewQuestions.ask: Do you want basic released-value coverage or full-value protection?
interviewQuestions.why: Released value is $0.60/lb — most people do not realise how low that is.

interviewQuestions.ask: Anything else that would slow a crew down — narrow doors, a gate code, a loading dock reservation, parking permits?
interviewQuestions.why: Access surprises are billed hourly on the day.

priceModel.notes: Transparent reference model used to build the fair-price benchmark and to give counterparty agents an internal cost basis. Not shown to counterparties.

redFlagRules.label: More than 30% below fair-market benchmark
redFlagRules.explain: A quote this far below the benchmark is a warning sign, not a win. It usually means fees are being withheld until moving day.
redFlagRules.citation: FMCSA: sight-unseen and underpriced estimates are 40% more likely to change on moving day.

redFlagRules.label: More than 40% above fair-market benchmark
redFlagRules.explain: Priced well above the benchmark for this job. Usually recoverable with a competing quote.

redFlagRules.label: Non-binding estimate
redFlagRules.explain: A non-binding estimate can legally rise on moving day. Binding or not-to-exceed is the protection you want.
redFlagRules.citation: BBB logs 13,000+ mover complaints a year, largely unexpected price hikes.

redFlagRules.label: Add-on fees only disclosed when pushed
redFlagRules.explain: Fees that only appear when you ask are the mechanism behind the 30%+ inflated final bill.

redFlagRules.label: Deposit above 20% of total
redFlagRules.explain: Large upfront deposits are associated with hostage-load disputes, which have nearly tripled since 2017.

redFlagRules.label: No USDOT / state license number given
redFlagRules.explain: Licensed interstate and intrastate movers can produce a USDOT or state number on request.

redFlagRules.label: Released-value coverage only ($0.60/lb)
redFlagRules.explain: $0.60 per pound means a 50 lb TV is covered for $30. Ask what full-value protection costs.

negotiationLevers.label: Price match a real competing quote
negotiationLevers.script: I have an itemised quote from {competitor} at {competitorTotal} for this exact job. Can you match or beat it?

negotiationLevers.label: Waive weekend / peak-date surcharge
negotiationLevers.script: Can you take the weekend surcharge off if I keep the date?

negotiationLevers.label: Trade date flexibility for price
negotiationLevers.script: If I move mid-week instead, what does that do to the number?

negotiationLevers.label: Get packing or materials included
negotiationLevers.script: If I stay at your price, will you include the packing materials?

negotiationLevers.label: Convert to binding / not-to-exceed
negotiationLevers.script: Can you put that in writing as a not-to-exceed number?

negotiationLevers.label: Reduce or remove deposit
negotiationLevers.script: Can we do this with no deposit, card on file instead?

negotiationLevers.label: Full-value protection included
negotiationLevers.script: Include full-value protection at that price and I will book today.

callPolicy.disclosure: Hi, I should say upfront — I'm an AI assistant calling on behalf of a customer, {customerName}, who is getting quotes for a move. Is it alright if I run through the details with you?
callPolicy.robotAnswer: Yes, I am — I'm an AI assistant, and {customerName} is a real customer with a real move on {moveDate}. I have the full inventory in front of me and I can book today if the number works.
honestyConstraints: Never invent inventory, dimensions, or access details that are not in the confirmed JobSpec.
Never cite a competing quote that is not persisted in the FairMove store with a conversation ID.
Never claim a quote is binding unless the counterparty said so.
Never misrepresent the move date, the customer's flexibility, or a deadline.
Disclose AI status immediately when asked, and do not deflect.

counterparties.voiceProfile: warm, consultative, always adding a package

outcomePolicy.statement: We don't give prices over the phone — we do a walkthrough first, that's just our policy.

callListProvenance.note: In production this list is built programmatically from Google Places / Yelp Fusion by category + ZIP radius. The schema below mirrors the Places response so the swap is a fetch, not a refactor.

reportCopy.headline: Same job, same fees, ranked honestly.
